mission.title: consul: Connect: Envoy sidecars enter state where they fail to receive xds updates from Consul
mission.description: Connect: Envoy sidecars enter state where they fail to receive xds updates from Consul. This issue affects 19+ users.

steps.title: Identify consul troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your consul deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `"rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"`

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
We'll see this in a specific Envoy sidecar on a Nomad Client. For this detail, we'll say Service A (svc-a) is talking to Service B (svc-b). IPs/names are redacted here but we can provide more info securely via email.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Connect: Envoy sidecars enter state where they fail to…
steps.description:
The `resourceVersion` map was doing two jobs prior to this PR. The first job was to track what version of every resource we know envoy currently has. The second was to track subscriptions to those resources (by way of the empty string for a version). This mostly works out fine, but occasionally
```yaml
$ consul info
agent:
	check_monitors = 1
	check_ttls = 39
	checks = 159
	services = 123
build:
	version = 1.10.0
consul:
	acl = disabled
	known_servers = 3
	server = false
runtime:
	arch = amd64
	cpu_count = 12
	goroutines = 2096
	max_procs = 12
	os = linux
	version = go1.16.5
serf_lan:
	coordinate_resets = 0
	encrypted = false
	event_queue = 0
	event_time = 106
	failed = 0
	health_score = 0
	intent_queue = 0
	left = 0
	member_time = 67643
	members = 230
	query_queue = 0
	query_time = 4
```

steps.title: Upgrade consul to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade consul:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade consul consul/consul --namespace consul
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul
helm list -n consul
```

steps.title: Confirm Connect: Envoy sidecars enter state where they… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `"rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: The `resourceVersion` map was doing two jobs prior to this PR. The first job was to track what version of every resource we know envoy currently has. The second was to track subscriptions to those resources (by way of the empty string for a version). This mostly works out fine, but occasionally leads to consul removing a resource and accidentally (effectively) unsubscribing at the same time.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
